summary: Completed testing of dashboard filter features: Active/Completed toggle, date range filters (Today, This Week, Next Week, This Month, Next Month, Overdue, Custom), and column height fix. All backend API tests passed (11/11). Frontend UI tests confirmed all filter buttons are present and functional.
passed_tests: Backend: status_filter=active returns only non-completed tasks
Backend: status_filter=completed returns only completed tasks
Backend: Default filter behaves like active filter
Backend: date_from and date_to filter tasks by due date
Backend: This week date range filter works
Backend: Next month date range filter works
Backend: Combined status and date filters work together
Backend: Empty date range returns 0 tasks
Backend: Dashboard response structure is valid
Backend: Task response structure is valid
Frontend: Active Tasks toggle visible and selected by default
Frontend: Completed Tasks toggle visible and clickable
Frontend: All date filter buttons present (all, overdue, today, this_week, next_week, this_month, next_month, custom)
Frontend: Grid has 'items-start' class for independent column heights
Frontend: Columns have different heights (210px, 210px, 301px) confirming items-start works
critical_code_review_comments: ESLint warning: useEffect in TaskHub.js has missing dependency 'fetchDashboard' - consider adding to dependency array or using useCallback
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dashboard filter features fully implemented and tested. Backend /api/dashboard accepts status_filter (active/completed/all), date_from, date_to query params. Frontend TaskHub.js has viewMode state for Active/Completed toggle, dateFilter state for date shortcuts, and grid uses items-start for independent column heights. Onboarding popup appears on dashboard but doesn't block functionality.
